title: Scale cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: the stronger scale setting should materially change framing/detail on decoded media
the same scale sweep should materially increase exported decoded-media crossing density on the rendered mix

expectedVideo: the decoded fixture should zoom cleanly without freezing or exposing undefined edges
larger scale values should materially change framing on the committed clip
expectedAudio: fixture audio should pitch-shift with the same scale law as the osc case
the rendered mix should stay stable with no silence or obvious glitching
manualChecks: Does scale remain coupled and usable on decoded media as well as procedural osc?
Does the stronger setting still read as deliberate zoom plus pitch shift rather than codec damage?
